author: @tonyponsford
text:
Hi Rob, trust you are well.
Need to throw something to you to play with coz it could be either very important or the signs i should give up this stuff..
Ok, we know battery internal resistance is mega important...say i want to make a 48V array out of 4 x12 V batteries..no worries, poz to neg to poz and off you go.
Now, say i have 200Ahr 12V batteries and i want to make a 600Ahr 48V array?
Common config would be to create 4x12V batteries and then parallel 3 banks, yes!!
But, if you parallel 3 batteries first, ie 12V 600Ahr, and THEN couple them in series with the other groups, you have effectively reduced the internal resistance by an order of magnitude !!
Same voltage, same amperage but substantially lower effective internal res. IF i am right then we can open source this !!!
I have lost sleep over this, tell me i am wrong !!   All the best. Tony, the thick Brummie.

replies.author: @tonyponsford
replies.text:
hello Rob, have you read this and probably your busy...in which can you please ackn so i know its been received :))
thank you Tony.

author: @Kiyarose3999
text: That’s a coincidence, I was going to share a vid of Bamboo Bikes after seeing you use Bamboo for the Quad. It would be nice to see the joints wrapped using Bamboo strips as is traditionally done. But Jute is a lot better environmentally than Carbon fibre etc. I wonder how you made the Bamboo fit snugly into the metal joints? As they looked a lot different diameter.?

text: Another way to make the fitted pipe joint is to chuck up a sanding drum that’s the same diameter as the pipe you’re fitting to and sand a notch in the end of the fitted pipe. 👍🏻

author: @The411
text: Interesting! How did you attach the 45's from the rear wheel to the seatpost upright? Looks like weight on the rear wheel might try push those past the seatpost? (and folding the bike)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: peg and stitch joints mate - same as used in bamboo roofing applications

replies.author: @mulgerbill
replies.text:
Gotta admit to a bit of worry about that junction myself. Seatstays to seat tube faces some of the highest dynamic loading any bike frame will see.
Why most builders run the stays right up to the height of the top tube so there's a counter loading
Plenty of speculation on my part tho, I've not used bamboo for bike applications
